messages.content: Project is done. Shipped 2 weeks late. Got 38 signups (target was 50). 8 paying customers (target was 10). The scope creep with expense tracking cost me about 2 weeks. The core invoice flow is solid.
You've shipped Client Invoice Tracker. Walk me through what you actually built — what does a user get right now?

A web app to create client profiles, generate invoices with line items, send via email, and download as PDF. Payments are tracked manually — they mark invoices paid. There's a dashboard showing outstanding invoices and total owed. No mobile app, no Stripe, no expense tracking — that got cut after the scope creep cost me 2 weeks.
Against your success criteria (50 freelancers sign up within 30 days) — where did you land? Be honest.

38 signups, 8 paying. Missed both. The late ship cost me the ProductHunt window — momentum was lower. Conversion to paid is below what I expected; I think people don't see the value until they invoice a real client, and my onboarding doesn't guide them to that moment.
What was the single biggest thing that slowed you down across the whole project?

Expense tracking. A beta user asked for it, I said yes without validating it further. It took 2 full weeks and it's the least-used feature in the app. I told myself it would improve conversions, but I never checked that assumption with anyone else.
What's the most important thing you learned about yourself as a builder?

I respond too eagerly to individual feature requests. One person asking for something feels like signal, but it's noise until I hear it from five people. I need to log requests and look for patterns before building.
What's going in the v2 backlog beyond the items I already have deferred?

Stripe integration for online payment collection, recurring invoice templates, and a proper onboarding flow that walks someone through their first invoice end-to-end. Also mobile-responsive styling — it's desktop-only right now and I've had complaints.

project.title: Client Invoice Tracker
project.one_liner: A lightweight SaaS for freelancers to create and track client invoices
project.core_problem: Freelancers lose hours chasing late payments
